data.title: AI-Related Stocks Reach Record 45% of S&P 500 Market Cap
data.description: AI-related stocks have reached a new milestone, now comprising 45% of the S&P 500 market capitalization. According to NS3.AI, this significant growth highlights the increasing influence of artificial intelligence in the financial markets. Additionally, a report from the Kobeissi Letter indicates that 15.4% of investment-grade debt is now associated with AI, with AI-linked debt totaling $1.4 trillion. This trend underscores the expanding role of AI in shaping investment strategies and market dynamics.

data.title: The largest ASTER long position on the chain, address 0x1527, was converted to a long position in CHIP.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, the largest ASTER long position address on the chain, 0x1527, currently holds long positions in CHIP, as well as long positions in MON and TAO. Position details: 24.93 million ASTER (US$16.72 million), 36.18 million CHIP (US$3.43 million), and 81.55 million MON (US$2.52 million).

data.title: The Smarter Web Company Increases Bitcoin Holdings
data.description: London-listed technology firm, The Smarter Web Company, has increased its Bitcoin holdings by acquiring an additional 44 Bitcoins. According to Foresight News, this acquisition brings the company's total Bitcoin holdings to 2,750.

data.title: Legal Analysis on Liability Clauses and DAO Governance Responsibilities
data.description: A recent legal analysis explores the effectiveness of liability clauses and the responsibilities associated with DAO governance. According to PANews, the study delves into the implications of fault grouping and how these legal frameworks impact decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs). The analysis highlights the complexities involved in DAO governance, particularly in relation to legal accountability and the enforcement of liability clauses. It examines the challenges faced by DAOs in navigating legal responsibilities and the potential consequences of inadequate governance structures. The report underscores the importance of understanding legal obligations within the context of decentralized systems, offering insights into how DAOs can better manage their governance and liability issues.

data.title: Bitcoin's Long-Term Holders Increase Supply Despite Price Drop
data.description: Bitcoin's long-term holders have significantly increased their supply in the first quarter, according to a report by ARK Invest. According to BlockBeats, the supply held by these steadfast investors surged by 69%, rising from 2.13 million BTC to 3.6 million BTC. This increase occurred despite a 22% decline in Bitcoin's price, reaching levels not seen since 2020.

data.title: Bitget launches its 20th VIP Regular Airdrop Program; this round's airdrop project is preSPAX.
data.description: Bitget is launching the 20th installment of its "VIP Airdrop Extravaganza," running from April 24th at 8:00 PM to May 7th at 8:00 PM (UTC+8). This installment includes two parts: a VIP privilege prize pool and a VIP challenge prize pool. VIP3 and above users who register and maintain their VIP level until the end of the event can share a prize pool of 80 preSPAX tokens. VIP1 and above users who register and complete the corresponding tasks can share a prize pool of 100 preSPAX tokens. For more details, please refer to the official Bitget platform.

data.title: Advisor to Iran's Supreme Leader: Iran's unity is Trump's "nightmare"
data.description: On March 24, local time, Mokhber, an advisor to Iran's Supreme Leader, said on social media that Iranian officials were united, the Iranian people were full of pride and patriotism, and that the soldiers of the Iranian armed forces had sacrificed their lives for their country, a "nightmare for losers." Mokhber wrote, "Faced with 90 million Iranian revolutionaries, Trump has no choice but to incite division." Earlier on March 23, US President Trump posted on social media that Iran's "hardliners" and "moderates" were "fighting amongst themselves." (CCTV News)

data.title: A whale opened a long position in Brent crude oil after making a profit of $1.93 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, whale "0xb58" closed its long position in $BRENTOIL, realizing a profit of $1.93 million, and subsequently reopened a 3x long position in $BRENTOIL with 50,000 units. The whale still holds a 3x short position in SOL, with a floating loss of $2.23 million.

data.title: MGBX will launch spot trading for Asteroid Shiba (ASTEROID) and OpenGradient (OPG).
data.description: According to official news, MGBX will list Asteroid Shiba (ASTEROID) and OpenGradient (OPG) spot trading on April 24, 2026 at 18:00 (SGT). Deposits will open at 16:00 (SGT) on April 24, 2026. Trading will open at 18:00 (SGT) on April 24, 2026. Withdrawals will open at 19:00 (SGT) on April 26, 2026.

data.title: Planet Midday News
data.description: 1. Metaplanet issued 8 billion yen worth of zero-coupon bonds to increase its Bitcoin holdings; 2. MegaETH officially announced its TGE on April 30th, with Polymarket reporting a 95% probability of issuance on that day; 3. Polymarket is cracking down on insider trading: if users are found to be using government secrets for trading, they will be handed over to the Department of Justice for investigation; 4. Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a net inflow of $223 million yesterday, marking the eighth consecutive day of net inflows; 5. The rsETH incident caused Aave's deposit size to decrease by approximately 37%, but the outflow of funds has now slowed; 6. The market capitalization of ETH chain Meme coin FLORK briefly broke a new high, rising over 100% intraday; 7. Riot Platforms transferred another 500 BTC to NYDIG for sale; 8. The ten-day streak of net inflows ended, with Ethereum spot ETFs experiencing a net outflow of $75.936 million yesterday.

data.title: This week's top mining news: Bitcoin mining company TeraWulf completes approximately $1.035 billion in rights issue.
data.description: Week 16 of 2026 (April 11-April 17): 1. According to Cloverpool, the average hashrate of the Bitcoin network was 935 EH/s, with a high of 1064 EH/s and a low of 815 EH/s, a decrease of 4.43% compared to the average hashrate of the previous week (978.9 EH/s). 2. According to blockchain.com, the average price of Bitcoin was $76,048.3, with a high of $79,523 and a low of $73,741.5, an increase of 3.9% compared to the average price of the previous week ($68,863). 3. Mining news worth noting includes: (1) Mining companies are transforming from Bitcoin mining to AI, and the stock prices of Keel and Hive have risen; (2) Alcoa plans to sell its idle smelter to NYDIG, and Bitcoin mining companies are accelerating their deployment of energy infrastructure; (3) Bitcoin mining company TeraWulf has completed a rights issue of approximately US$1.035 billion; Data partner: Cango Inc. (CANG), a Bitcoin mining company listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Introduces Stablecoin Reserve Portfolio for Issuers
data.description: Morgan Stanley has unveiled a Stablecoin Reserve Portfolio, allowing stablecoin issuers to deposit reserves of $10 million or more into its MSNXX money market fund to earn interest. According to NS3.AI, the fund is structured to ensure stability and liquidity by investing in short-term government bonds and cash equivalents. This initiative aligns with the proposed GENIUS framework for stablecoin regulation, aiming to provide a compliant and secure option for stablecoin issuers.

data.title: Bitcoin: Digital Gold and Future Global Settlement Asset
data.description: Bitcoin is often referred to as both digital gold and a future global settlement asset. According to PANews, this dual role highlights its significance in the evolving financial landscape. As digital gold, Bitcoin is seen as a store of value, akin to precious metals, offering a hedge against inflation and economic instability. Meanwhile, its potential as a global settlement asset suggests its capability to facilitate international transactions efficiently. This dual functionality positions Bitcoin uniquely in the financial ecosystem, attracting interest from both investors and institutions.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Show Resilience Amid U.S. Pressure, Former Ambassador Says
data.description: Former U.S. Ambassador to Bahrain, Adam Ereli, shared insights on Iran's resilience in the face of U.S. and Israeli pressures, according to Jin10 citing Al Jazeera. Ereli highlighted the 'revolutionary fervor' of Iran's Revolutionary Guards, suggesting their ability to endure hardship surpasses the expectations of many U.S. policymakers. While the duration of Tehran's endurance under U.S.-imposed 'siege conditions' remains uncertain, Ereli believes it could be longer than anticipated, particularly regarding yielding to American demands. He emphasized Iran's sense of pride and survival instinct, framing the situation as a life-or-death struggle for Iran, contrasting it with what he described as a 'war of choice' for the U.S., which could withdraw without significant consequences.

data.title: Web3 Platform DeBox to Cease vBOX Service Amid Business Adjustments
data.description: Web3 social platform DeBox has announced the termination of its vBOX service due to business adjustments. According to Foresight News, users are required to withdraw their vBOX points to the on-chain BOX by April 30 at 23:59 UTC+8. Points not withdrawn by the deadline will be frozen, and future withdrawals will only be possible through manual application, incurring additional processing fees and extended transaction times.

data.title: Japan to Release Oil Reserves Amid Iran Crisis
data.description: Japan's Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry announced on April 24 that the country will release 5.8 million kiloliters of its national oil reserves in response to the Iran crisis. According to BlockBeats, the release of a portion of these reserves is set to begin on May 1. This move aims to stabilize the energy supply amid ongoing geopolitical tensions.

data.title: Meta's Planned Layoffs Influence Polymarket Predictions
data.description: Polymarket's prediction market, "Tech Layoffs Up or Down in 2026?", saw a significant increase to 85% YES on Wednesday. According to NS3.AI, this surge follows reports that Meta intends to reduce its workforce by approximately 8,000 employees. The initial phase of these layoffs is scheduled for May 20. Additionally, Meta's AI capital expenditure guidance for 2026 is projected to be between $115 billion and $135 billion.

data.title: South Korea's KOSDAQ Index Surges Past 1,200 Points
data.description: South Korea's KOSDAQ index experienced a significant rise, reaching 1,200.22, marking an increase of 2.21% from the previous day. According to NS3.AI, this surge pushed the index above the 1,200-point threshold. Meanwhile, the benchmark KOSPI index traded at 6,442.84, reflecting a decline of 0.51% after achieving its all-time high of 6,557.76 the day before.

data.title: U.S. Senator Elizabeth Warren Seeks Details on Erebor Bank's Regulatory Approval
data.description: U.S. Senator Elizabeth Warren is seeking further information regarding Erebor Bank, according to Foresight News. The bank, co-founded by a prominent supporter of U.S. President Donald Trump, focuses on cryptocurrency operations and received regulatory approval earlier this year. Warren has expressed significant concerns based on a fundraising document obtained by her office, questioning how the bank secured its license within months and whether political connections expedited the process.

data.title: The state of Wisconsin has sued Kalshi and other platforms, claiming that prediction markets constitute unlicensed gambling.
data.description: Wisconsin Attorney General Josh Kaul has filed a lawsuit against Kalshi, Coinbase, Polymarket, Robinhood, and Crypto.com, alleging that their prediction markets are essentially unlicensed gambling activities. The complaint claims that the platforms' marketing methods and event contract pricing structures meet the state's definition of gambling. This case further exacerbates the conflict between state-level regulation and the federal jurisdiction of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). (CoinDesk)
